finding.summary: No clear problem. The dry lead vocal shows healthy headroom (true peak -5.5 dB, sample peak -6.0 dB), uncompressed dynamics (crest factor 12.0 dB, LRA 8.0 LU), and a capture with only 5% silence so the metrics are meaningful. The send to Vocal Verb at +4.0 dB is the only processing path and is not in this payload.
finding.probable_cause: Track is intentionally dry (no inserts), and the post-FX audio is consistent with an unprocessed vocal at sensible levels; the 12 dB tilt favoring 100 Hz over 4 kHz is within natural vocal range and not supported as a defect by the available spectrum points.
finding.confidence_reason: Isolation is verified and silence_fraction is low, so levels and spectrum are trustworthy. The tonal tilt observation is based on only three 1/3-octave points; without more bands or knowing the vocalist/genre, a corrective move cannot be safely justified.

evidence_refs.description: True peak -5.5 dB indicates healthy headroom.
Crest factor 12.0 dB indicates no overcompression.
LRA 8.0 LU consistent with moderate, non-squashed dynamics.
Silence fraction 0.05 means the capture is mostly signal; metrics are not diluted by dead air.
Isolation verified, so measured levels reflect this track only.
Reverb is handled on a separate track (Vocal Verb) not measured here; this capture cannot verify the wet balance.
Three 1/3-octave points at 100 Hz, 1 kHz, 4 kHz; not enough resolution to justify a corrective EQ move.

proposal.reason: No clear problem is supported by this capture, and no safe move can be justified from the available evidence.
